action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 20's local test showed the pathfinder branch was active but episodes either stayed far away (Ep 0: 5.64m avg, 750 steps) or ended very early when close (Ep 1: 220 steps, Ep 2: 91 steps). Early termination strongly correlates with finding the human, suggesting collision or proximity-based episode termination. The best 50-episode eval so far was iteration 11 (GPS-only, no pathfinder): 82 safe steps, 0.6 nav_to_pos_success. Analysis: (1) The predict.py contract may not be fully satisfied; I need to verify it passes `env` to the model and computes safe_follow_steps manually. (2) The pathfinder should only activate when geodesic distance exceeds Euclidean by a meaningful margin (wall in the way), otherwise GPS-only is sufficient and less error-prone. (3) Early termination likely comes from aggressive close-range interaction; I need to see if it happens with the current model. Decision: Write a clean predict.py and a refined model.py (pathfinder only when geo > euc + 0.5 or geo > 3.0, conservative idx=1 lookahead, forward modulation during turns). Run train.py to save checkpoint, then run a 5-episode local pipeline test (test_predict.py) to check for early termination and avg distance. Do NOT submit eval this iteration; use the local test output to decide whether to fix the controller or submit next iteration.
